There are what I suspect are elderberry canes in the bush that lines the sidewalk. Needs verification. Access: Public.
There are several grape vines that give fruit around here during the fall. When I tried them in early September, they were still somewhat sour. They were sparse also. Check for pesticides.
565 Coventry Ln, Buffalo Grove, IL 60089, USA
Large amount of frost grapes near the bridge, very tasty. Watch out for possible pesticide contamination. Access: Public.
Several small groves of black raspberries on this stretch of the bike trail. Need species ID verification. Access: Public.

There's a grove of serviceberry trees around this area that bear quality fruit. Be careful for pesticides/pollution, and make sure to wash fruits carefully. Access: Public. Falling Fruit season: May-July.
403 Woodbury Rd, Buffalo Grove, IL 60089, USA

Sweet cherry tree on private property, however with some fruit hanging over the small foot trail, which you can legally harvest. The flavor was good in the fruit that weren't damaged by birds. Falling Fruit season: June-unknown.
